name: Graph Coloring Problem
description: Assign one of k colors to each vertex so adjacent vertices have different colors.

details.summary: Find a valid vertex coloring using at most k colors.
learningGoals: Understand adjacency constraints in graph coloring.
See how backtracking searches for a valid color assignment.
Recognize why the problem becomes hard as the graph grows.
notes: Vertices are entered as comma-separated names.
Edges are entered one per line using A-B or A,B.
The solver uses exact backtracking, so large dense graphs may take longer.

complexity.time: O(k^n) in the worst case for exact backtracking
complexity.space: O(n + m) for the graph plus recursion state

inputs.label: Edges
inputs.description: One edge per line, e.g. A-B.
